Regression from Formula with Robust Standard Errors

Source: R/utils.R
reg_from_formula.Rd

Fits a linear regression model from a formula (or formula string) with optional weights and computes HC1 robust standard errors. When cluster_id is provided, computes cluster-robust (CR1) standard errors using sandwich::vcovCL instead of heteroskedasticity-robust (HC1) standard errors.

Usage

reg_from_formula(formula, data, weights = NULL, cluster_id = NULL)

Arguments

formula

Formula object or character string representing the regression formula

data

Data frame containing the regression data

weights

Optional numeric vector of weights for weighted regression. Must have the same length as the number of rows in data.

cluster_id

Optional vector identifying clusters (e.g., individual IDs in panel data). When provided, cluster-robust standard errors are computed via sandwich::vcovCL. When NULL (default), heteroskedasticity- robust (HC1) standard errors are computed.

Value

List containing:

  • coef: Coefficient test results with robust standard errors

  • vcov: Robust variance-covariance matrix (HC1 or cluster-robust)

  • model_matrix: Model matrix from the regression

  • residuals: Regression residuals

  • fitted: Fitted values
